summary: Check reads every kind's surface member document through the one generic Unit loader, retiring the skill/rule IR→Unit adapter on the check path

per.section: A built-in kind is an adapter — two faces

edit.description: Collapse the per-kind `skill_features`/`rule_features` dispatch in `gate`/`why`/`requirements` into one generic loop: load each built-in kind's surface members via `Unit::from_surface_dir` (the loader custom kinds already use) and run its embedded `skill`/`rule` KIND.md extraction (`builtin_kind::definition`), so built-in and custom kinds read through one path. The typed `Workspace` is kept for the adapter-face callers (drift/bundle/apply).

Retire `skill_unit`/`rule_unit` (the IR→Unit adapters) and reshape `skill_features`/`rule_features` to run the embedded extraction over a generically-loaded `Unit` rather than a typed IR, keeping the permissive unknown-key fold. Engine-code IR parse/emit stays only for import/apply/drift, not the check read. Update this module's tests.

Provide the generic per-kind surface-Unit loader the gate reads (mirroring `main::custom_units`), keeping the typed `skills`/`rules` fields on `Workspace` intact so drift/bundle/apply (the adapter faces) are unaffected. Update the `Workspace::load` tests to assert the generic read yields the same features.

Only if `Workspace` is restructured beyond keeping its typed fields: keep the drift/apply/re-add adapter faces on typed IRs (reload `Skill`/`Rule` where needed). No change expected if `Workspace` keeps `skills`/`rules`.
Only if `Workspace` is restructured: keep the skill/rule counts working off whatever `Workspace` exposes. No change expected if the typed fields survive.

tests.asserts: loading a written skill/rule surface yields the same extracted features through the generic `Unit` loader that the retired IR→Unit adapter produced.
`skill_features`/`rule_features` computed over a generically-loaded `Unit` still fold unknown `[clause.*]` keys and surface `satisfies` off the surface, matching the prior IR-path output.

acceptance: `temper check` extracts skill/rule features by reading their surface member documents through the same generic `Unit` loader custom kinds use — no IR→Unit adapter on the check path; the typed IRs survive only for import/apply/drift; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Read-path unification tail — `(extraction-source-not-mechanism)`, RESOLVED. The skill/rule surface member document is `[clause.*]`-headed (`Skill::to_document`), so `Unit::from_surface_dir` already lifts its fields — the generic read is feasible. Keep `Workspace` typed for drift/bundle/apply; only the gate's feature extraction goes generic. IMPORT-DISCOVERY-GENERIC is serialized after this (shared drift.rs).

summary: Import discovers built-in kinds off their embedded KIND.md `governs` locus, retiring the hardwired `discover_skill_dirs`/`discover_rule_files` scans

per.section: A built-in kind is an adapter — two faces

edit.description: Drive built-in-kind discovery off the embedded `skill`/`rule` KIND.md `governs` (`builtin_kind::definition`) through one generic scanner, retiring the hardwired `discover_skill_dirs`/`discover_rule_files`. Generalize the `governs` scanner so a subdir glob (`*/SKILL.md`) and a flat glob (`*.md`) both resolve — today `discover_kind_units` globs immediate files only. Preserve the bare-harness-is-a-skill special case (a root `SKILL.md`) and keep the existing import tests green.
Update the drift `added`-axis rescan to call the unified `governs`-keyed discovery for built-in kinds too, replacing the retired `discover_skill_dirs`/`discover_rule_files` calls.
Update the two `import::discover_skill_dirs`/`discover_rule_files` call sites (schema-modeline placement) to the unified `governs`-keyed discovery.

tests.asserts: `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (subdir glob) and `.claude/rules/*.md` (flat glob) both discover off the embedded KIND.md `governs`; the bare-root skill still imports; import stays idempotent (byte-identical re-run).

acceptance: `temper import` discovers `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` and `.claude/rules/*.md` by keying off the embedded built-in KIND.md `governs` through one scanner shared with custom kinds, the bare-root skill case still imports, and drift/install reuse the same discovery; idempotence snapshot holds; gates green.
notes: Read-path unification tail, discovery half. Embedded `skill`/`rule` KIND.md now carry `governs` (`.claude/skills` + `*/SKILL.md`; `.claude/rules` + `*.md`), so discovery keys off the declaration like custom kinds. Wrinkle: `discover_kind_units` globs immediate files only, so the skill's `*/SKILL.md` subdir glob needs the scanner generalized. Serialized after SURFACE-READING-GENERIC (shared drift.rs).

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; the surface-language and read-path unification it would ride have shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent typed IR — the adapter-face model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed frontmatter (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved in `extra`, content-faithful body, provenance, `to_document`/`from_dir`. Used by the import parse and apply emit faces (the check read goes through the generic `Unit` loader post-SURFACE-READING-GENERIC).

The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (read side), beside `kinds/skill` and `kinds/rule`: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. Embedded by build.rs like the other built-in kinds — no engine-code extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib by build.rs (the product `packages/` tree, not a `.temper/` member). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Add `"agent"` to `BUILTIN_KINDS` so a `[kind.agent]` registration reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const and register it in the built-in package map beside `skill.anthropic`/`rule.anthropic`.

Bind the agent package floor and include `agent` in the gate's generic by-kind loop and the schema `floors` list; the members read through the generic `Unit` loader, so no hand-coded feature fn is added. Wire the import/apply adapter faces to `src/agent.rs`.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred — more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; revive only if a story demands it. Rewritten to the current architecture: `agent` is a typed IR (adapter faces) + embedded KIND.md + `agent.anthropic` package like skill/rule, read through the generic loader — no per-kind Workspace field. On revival, reconcile the exact file set against then-current code and serialize the shared-file edits (lib/kind/builtin/main) against any open entry.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md — the two missing launch community docs (two-sided AI-authorship disclosure; vuln-reporting evidence bar)

per.section: The community surface — small, honest, gated

gate.reason: build's writable fence (.flume/chain.ts BUILD_WRITABLE_PATHS) lists only README/CHANGELOG/AGENTS/LICENSE among root docs — no root CONTRIBUTING.md/SECURITY.md glob — so build would bail; a human must widen the fence (chain.ts is human territory) and enable private vulnerability reporting (a repo setting) before build can author these

new.description: Small and honest per the Decision: how to file a good issue, 'search first', PRs by prior discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (inbound AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure; the contributor must understand and defend the change without the assistant). Expectation-setting, not an invitation to co-maintain.

Vulnerability-reporting policy with an explicit evidence bar — a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the 'unverified AI-generated vuln reports are the new spam' lesson). Points at private vulnerability reporting (the GitHub repo setting a human enables).

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md exist at root, matching specs/55's launch-set Decisions (two-sided disclosure; demonstrate-not-speculate); the fence has been widened so build's entry-fence preflight admits them.
notes: Launch set (specs/55). Already on disk: README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE-MIT/APACHE, issue forms (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,config}.yml). Missing only CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md. Both are root-level per the spec but outside build's root-docs fence — parked on a human fence-widen (chain.ts) + enabling private reporting. Parked entries are skipped by the entry-fence gate, so this files cleanly.
